title: The video game industry: GTA VI gets more expensive, Half-Life 3 shows signs, and Unreal Engine loses an evangelist
summary: Top news from the world of video games includes the €120 price tag for GTA VI, a possible hint of Half-Life 3, the departure of a high-ranking executive from Epic Games, the trailer for Netflix's new movie, and a curious artificial intelligence in Age of Empires II.

title: Ban on AI in Norwegian schools and studies warning of its academic risks
summary: Norway becomes the first country to ban the use of artificial intelligence in primary education, while research indicates that AI inflates grades and can harm learning.

title: Cybersecurity operations: Canada cleans bots, INTERPOL warns of scams, and massive data theft in Texas
summary: The Canadian intelligence agency removes infected bots, malware is discovered on 4,300 routers, INTERPOL warns of the rise in AI-powered scams, and Texas suffers the leak of 3 million IDs.

title: New technology regulations: UK bans social media for minors, Airbnb faces restrictions, and more
summary: The UK enacts a social media ban for minors, Kansas City implements facial recognition on buses, SpaceX receives the worst ESG rating, and Discord tests age verification, among other regulatory measures.

title: Open source AI models gain ground: GLM-5.2, Sakana Fugu, and Apertus compete with private ones
summary: Several open language models like GLM-5.2 and Sakana Fugu demonstrate capabilities comparable to proprietary ones, while interest in fine-tuning and sovereign models grows.

title: Growing skepticism towards AI: fear of power concentration, public rejection, and corporate cost-cutting
summary: Executives like Satya Nadella warn about the risks of AI concentration, public opinion turns against it, and companies like Amazon and Uber restrict its use due to high costs.

title: Samsung integrates ChatGPT Enterprise, iOS 27 adds AI features, and HBO Max tests AI-powered ads
summary: Tech companies incorporate artificial intelligence into their products: Samsung deploys ChatGPT Enterprise in Korea, Apple prepares AI features in iOS 27, and HBO Max experiments with interactive AI ads.

title: Scams and tech controversies: World Cup, Polymarket, and Orb under scrutiny
summary: Scams related to the World Cup increase, Polymarket is accused of paying for misleading content, and iris scanning company Orb faces an investigation for financial irregularities.

title: Tesla under scrutiny: manipulated data in its autonomous driving and a fatal accident revive doubts
summary: Tesla is accused of having presented manipulated data to obtain approval for its Full Self-Driving system, while a fatal accident with a car in 'autopilot' mode and GM's staff reductions mark the sector's current affairs.

title: Windows 11 and Android 17 updates cause bugs and slowdowns on devices
summary: The latest Windows 11 updates break the recycle bin, block Office and OneDrive, while Android 17 arrives on Pixels freezing the screen and causing touch issues.

title: Nothing cancels its CMF Phone 3 Pro and analysis of switching from Galaxy S23 to S26 Ultra
summary: Nothing confirms that there will be no CMF Phone 3 Pro due to the high cost of memory, while a guide analyzes whether it is worth upgrading from the Galaxy S23 Ultra to the S26 Ultra.

title: TypeScript 7.0, Deno Desktop, PowerFox, and other tools that make developers' lives easier
summary: TypeScript 7.0 arrives with a Go-based foundation and 10x faster compilations, Deno Desktop enables desktop applications, PowerFox is a minimalist browser, and guides on JSON-LD and identity verification in Claude are published.

title: Reflections on corporate culture: hypocrisy, crushed spirit, and careers without prior experience
summary: Articles on how the current generation fears challenging hypocrisy, a Meta executive admits that workers are disheartened, and an entrepreneur recounts how he runs a company without having had a traditional job.

title: SK Hynix surpasses Samsung, UK bonds in real time, and falling rents in New York
summary: SK Hynix becomes the most valuable company in Korea ahead of Samsung, the British bond market offers real-time data for the first time, and New York rents record increasing defaults.

title: Scientific discoveries: Russian nuclear missile is worse than thought, a Roman canal emerges in the Rhine, and Earth receives radioactive rain
summary: Science news includes MIT's confirmation of the danger of the 'flying Chernobyl', the discovery of a 300-year-old Roman canal, and the revelation that Earth has been receiving radioactive rain from supernovae for millions of years.

title: New drivers, price increases, and curiosities in PC hardware
summary: AMD prepares a price increase for its Radeon RX 9000, Valve leaks compatibility with FSR 4.1, Mesa updates NVK drivers with DLSS, and various hardware incidents such as a Ryzen 9 9900X that fails after a BIOS update or an RTX 4070 that survives hammer blows.

title: New tech products: MD+HTML Reader, Cloudflare Temporary Accounts, MediaSeg, and more
summary: Product Hunt features five notable launches: an MD+HTML reader, temporary Cloudflare accounts, a media segmenter, an AI tool for flights, and a forge selector.

title: Best deals before Prime Day, Lidl's cooking robot, and the safest websites to download software
summary: Xataka compiles the best pre-Prime Day deals from Amazon, Lidl's smart cooking robot drops to a minimum price, and a list of the best websites to download free apps safely is published.

title: Ozempic curbs impulsivity, the body protects itself from heat by suppressing hunger, and AirPods as concert earplugs
summary: It is discovered that Ozempic reduces violent impulsiveness, the body suppresses appetite at high temperatures as a survival mechanism, and AirPods become popular at concerts as hearing protectors.

title: Heatwave in Spain, axolotls on Mexican banknotes, and Singapore's pride in its tap water
summary: Various news: Spain suffers an extreme heatwave, Mexico promotes axolotl conservation on its currency, and Singapore celebrates the drinkability of its tap water.
